text: Visual Question Answering (VQA) is a field of study which deals with extracting answers from images by using a meaningful representation of the output in the form of natural language. VQA not only has tremendous scope in the area of robotic interaction, video surveillance as well as assistive technologies but also faces a major bottleneck in the form of low accuracy achieved by state of the art models. Typically, there are two main reasons because of which VQA models suffer in achieving accuracy - the image features are unable to capture the dynamics of a scene and on the other hand the text features are not capable of conveying the required amount of information. In this paper, we try to overcome these two major hindrances in the VQA models. For this, we come up with an enhanced version of VQA model by proposing a method which has two main components: i) A model to extract multi-scale deep features and fuse them optimally for images and ii) Multi-scale representation for texts at word, phrase and sentence levels. Through experiments on various datasets, we confirm that achieving enhanced performance in VQA can be done by using multi-scale deep feature fusion for the enhanced image and textual features.
